chapter_title.ar: باب ضَمَانِ مَا يُصِيبُ الدَّوَابُّ وَ مَا لَا ضَمَانَ فِيهِ مِنْ ذَلِكَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 42 – Responsibility of what hits the animals and what is not a responsibility regarding it from that

text: هُ سُئِلَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ يَسِيرُ عَلَى طَرِيقٍ مِنْ طُرُقِ الْمُسْلِمِينَ عَلَى دَابَّتِهِ فَتُصِيبُ بِرِجْلِهَا فَقَالَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ مَا أَصَابَتْ بِرِجْلِهَا وَ عَلَيْهِ مَا أَصَابَتْ بِيَدِهَا وَ إِذَا وَقَفَتْ فَعَلَيْهِ مَا أَصَابَتْ بِيَدِهَا وَ رِجْلِهَا وَ إِنْ كَانَ يَسُوقُهَا فَعَلَيْهِ مَا أَصَابَتْ بِيَدِهَا وَ رِجْلِهَا أَيْضاً .

en.hubeali: Yunus, from Muhammad Bin Sinan, from Al A’ala Bin Al Fazl,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> being asked about a man who travelled upon a road from the Muslim roads upon his animal, so it hit someone with its (hind) legs. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is nothing upon him what it hits by its (hind) legs, and when he pauses it, so upon him is what it hits by its hands (front legs) and its (hind) legs, and even if he is ushering it, so upon him is what it hits by its hands (front legs) and its (hind) legs, as well’.
